source_script_line: Static freezes on a sidewalk as a transit board clicks off-rhythm.
body_orientation: three-quarter right (facing up the street toward the receding storefronts)
head_direction: turned further right and slightly up, toward the flickering signage
eye_target: the malfunctioning sign/transit area up the street
stance: weight settling onto rear (left) leg mid-stride-stop, front (right) foot planted flat, back heel just lifting
left_hand: gripping satchel strap at his shoulder
right_hand: slightly raised, fingers open -- a startled half-gesture, not a full stop-sign
depth_layer: foreground-left sidewalk, in front of the storefront glow, left of the puddle streaked with sign reflections

ground_contact.surface: wet sidewalk pavers, just off the puddle edge
ground_contact.note: Placed left of the puddle rather than in it, per canon reference art showing his boots dry/grounded, not submerged -- avoids implying a wade/splash pose the script doesn't call for.

lighting.key_source: streetlamp_near (cool cyan-white, from upper-right of his position)
lighting.fill_source: signage_magenta (warm magenta bounce from screen-left storefront)
lighting.rim_light: thin cyan rim on his right/upper silhouette edge, facing the lamp

occlusion: rain layer in front of the character (existing plate rain is behind him only; a foreground rain pass is added so drops read as falling between camera and character)
no puddle overlap on the boots at this stance (dry pavers per ground_contact note) -- reserved for a future pose variant if the script calls for a puddle step

reflection.note: Restrained mirrored reflection in the wet sheet below-left of his stance -- consistent with the plate's own diffuse neon reflections. Boots themselves stay on dry-ish pavers; the reflection lives on the adjacent water sheet, which is physically correct for a standing figure next to a reflective surface.

asset_limitation: This is Static's neutral clean-base reference pose (three-quarter-ish standing, satchel visible), not a custom-generated freeze-mid-step pose -- ComfyUI is offline this session so no new pose could be rendered. Orientation/placement below is chosen to match this existing asset as closely as the scripted beat allows; a true bespoke pose render is flagged as follow-up work once generation is available again.
